question: A line plot shows Xs above a number line. The number 3 has 4 Xs above it. What does that mean?
hints: Each X represents one piece of data at that value.
solution: 4 Xs above 3 means 4 items in the data set have the value 3.

question: A line plot of shoe sizes shows: size 1 has 2 Xs, size 2 has 5 Xs, size 3 has 3 Xs. Which shoe size is most common?
hints: The value with the most Xs is the most common.
solution: Size 2 has 5 Xs, the most, so it is the most common.

question: A line plot shows: 5 has 1 X, 6 has 3 Xs, 7 has 2 Xs, 8 has 4 Xs. How many data points are there in total?
On a line plot, the value 4 has 0 Xs. What does that mean?
answer: no data points have the value 4
hints: 0 Xs means that value doesn't appear in the data.
solution: No items in the data set have the value 4.

question: You measured the lengths of 5 pencils in inches: 6, 7, 6, 8, 6. How many Xs would go above the number 6 on a line plot?
solution: 6 appears 3 times, so 3 Xs go above 6.

question: A line plot shows plant heights in inches: 3 (2 Xs), 4 (5 Xs), 5 (3 Xs), 6 (1 X). What is the most common height?
solution: 4 inches has 5 Xs, more than any other height. It is the mode.

question: A line plot shows hours of sleep: 7 (1 X), 8 (4 Xs), 9 (6 Xs), 10 (3 Xs), 11 (1 X). How many people were surveyed?
A line plot shows: 2 (3 Xs), 3 (0 Xs), 4 (5 Xs), 5 (2 Xs). What is the range of the data?
Data set: 4, 5, 5, 6, 6, 6, 7, 7, 8. How many Xs go above each number on a line plot?
hints: Count the frequency of each number.
solution: 4 appears 1 time, 5 appears 2 times, 6 appears 3 times, 7 appears 2 times, 8 appears 1 time.

question: A line plot of test scores shows: 70 (1 X), 80 (3 Xs), 90 (5 Xs), 100 (2 Xs). How many students scored 80 or above?
A line plot shows ages at a party: 5 (2 Xs), 6 (4 Xs), 7 (3 Xs), 8 (1 X). What is the sum of all the ages?
A line plot shows how many books students read: 1 (3 Xs), 2 (5 Xs), 3 (4 Xs), 4 (2 Xs), 5 (1 X). What is the total number of books read?
A line plot shows lengths in inches: 10 (1 X), 11 (2 Xs), 12 (4 Xs), 13 (2 Xs), 14 (1 X). What is the median length?
hints: Total data points: 10. The median is the average of the 5th and 6th values when sorted.
solution: 10 data points sorted: 10, 11, 11, 12, 12, 12, 12, 13, 13, 14. Middle values (5th and 6th): both 12. Median = 12.

question: Two line plots compare Class A and Class B quiz scores. Class A: 7 (2 Xs), 8 (3 Xs), 9 (5 Xs). Class B: 7 (4 Xs), 8 (4 Xs), 9 (2 Xs). Which class had a higher average score?
hints: Class A total: 7(2)+8(3)+9(5) = 83, 10 students, avg 8.3. Class B: 7(4)+8(4)+9(2) = 78, 10 students, avg 7.8.

question: You have 12 data values and are making a line plot. The data: 3, 5, 4, 3, 6, 5, 3, 4, 5, 5, 6, 4. How many Xs should be above each number?
hints: Count each: 3 appears 3 times, 4 appears 3 times, 5 appears 4 times, 6 appears 2 times.
